name: Cal Coin and Jewelry
description_short: La Mesa, CA coin dealer and jewelry buyer specializing in gold, silver, bullion, diamonds, and luxury watches. Free on-the-spot appraisals; cash paid immediately.
description_long:
Cal Coin and Jewelry is a precious metals and jewelry dealer based in La Mesa, California, serving the greater San Diego area. Owner Owen Kochur brings more than 19 years of hands-on industry experience — active in the coin and jewelry trade since approximately 2006, with the business formally registered in 2021. Operating from a private office suite at 9019 Park Plaza Dr., the shop is appointment-friendly and positioned as a locally owned alternative to chain pawn shops and national gold-buying operations.

The core offering is buying precious metals and jewelry directly from the public for immediate cash payment. Cal Coin purchases gold (jewelry, scrap, and bullion), silver (coins, rounds, bars, and 90% junk silver), platinum, diamonds, estate and designer jewelry, luxury and pocket watches, sterling silver flatware and tea sets, Native American and Art Deco jewelry, and graded coins certified by PCGS, NGC, or ANACS. All evaluations are free, conducted on the spot in a private office. The shop also sells coins, bullion, and collectibles through its own website and an active eBay storefront. Minor watch services — battery replacements starting at $5 and band adjustments — are performed while you wait. Minimum sell thresholds apply for bullion sellers: 10 oz minimum for .999 fine bullion and 10 face value for 90% silver.

With a 5.0 Google rating across 395 reviews and 82+ Yelp reviews, Cal Coin has built an unusually strong local reputation. The willingness to buy PCGS-, NGC-, and ANACS-certified numismatic coins sets it apart from general gold buyers who evaluate only melt value. The private office setting is a deliberate design choice — appraisal consultations are conducted away from a public counter, making the experience more comfortable for sellers bringing in higher-value or sentimental items. The eBay store extends its reach for buyers unable to visit in person.

Cal Coin and Jewelry's strengths are its experienced ownership, consistently high customer satisfaction, and breadth of accepted items. The key limitations are practical: the shop is closed on weekends and keeps strict weekday hours (10am–5pm, Monday through Friday), which is a real barrier for working adults. No pricing is published online — all quotes require an in-person visit or phone call. There is only one location serving the San Diego metro area. The business is not BBB accredited. For sellers with common gold jewelry, silver coins, or a watch needing a battery, the experience is straightforward. Those with large bullion positions or complex estate pieces should call ahead to confirm the shop can handle their volume and item types.
address: 9019 Park Plaza Dr E, La Mesa, CA 91942
pros: Owner Owen Kochur has 19+ years of industry experience, active in the trade since approximately 2006
5.0 Google rating from 395 reviews — an exceptionally high score for a local metals and jewelry dealer
Free same-appointment appraisals conducted privately in a dedicated office, not over a public counter
Buys PCGS, NGC, and ANACS certified numismatic coins — not limited to melt-value gold and silver
Active eBay store (ebay.com/str/lamesacoinjewelry) allows remote buying for non-local customers
Watch battery replacements available starting at $5 while you wait — no separate jeweler trip needed
82+ Yelp reviews supporting a consistent local reputation across multiple review platforms
cons: Closed on weekends — Monday through Friday 10am–5pm only, making visits difficult for working adults
No pricing published online; all buy/sell quotes require an in-person visit or phone call
Minimum sell thresholds apply: 10 oz minimum for .999 fine bullion, 10 face value for 90% silver
Single La Mesa location with no additional branches across San Diego County
Not BBB accredited; BBB letter/star rating not confirmed in available research
best_for: San Diego-area residents looking to sell inherited or unwanted gold jewelry, coins, or silver for immediate cash
Coin collectors seeking to buy or sell PCGS, NGC, or ANACS certified numismatic coins and bullion
Local watch owners needing affordable battery replacements or band adjustments without a dedicated jeweler
Estate liquidators or families handling diamond jewelry, luxury watches, or sterling silver flatware sets
diagnosis: Cal Coin and Jewelry is genuinely well-suited for San Diego-area residents who want an experienced, highly-rated local buyer for gold, silver, coins, or jewelry — especially those with certified numismatic coins or higher-value estate pieces where expertise and trust matter. The main caveat is operational: weekday-only hours (10am–5pm) and no published pricing mean you must plan a deliberate visit during business hours, and large bullion sellers should call ahead to confirm minimums.
services: Buy gold jewelry, scrap gold, and gold bullion bars, rounds, and coins
Buy silver coins, rounds, bars, and 90% junk silver (10 face value minimum)
Buy loose diamonds and diamond jewelry
Buy estate, designer, fine, and engagement ring jewelry
Buy luxury wristwatches and pocket watches/clocks
Buy sterling silver flatware and tea sets
Buy Native American and Art Deco jewelry
Buy PCGS, NGC, and ANACS certified graded coins
Sell coins, bullion, and collectibles via website and eBay store
Watch battery replacement (starting at $5) and band adjustments while you wait
